title: Recommendations are completely off for my skin type
review: I filled out the entire skin profile when I signed up - dry skin
version: rosacea-prone. The app keeps recommending me alcohol-based toners and exfoliating acids. I had a flare-up last week because I trusted it. The personalization engine is clearly broken or not being used at all.

title: Reminders never fire at the right time
review: I set my morning routine reminder for 7:30am. It goes off at 9:15am every single day. By then I've already rushed out the door without doing my routine. What's the point of reminders if they're an hour and a half late?
author: busy_morning_user

title: Progress photos just disappear
review: I've been taking weekly progress photos for three months. Last Tuesday I opened the app and literally half of them were gone. I had 12 photos
version: no error message. This is months of my skin journey just vanished.

title: App crashes when I try to log retinol
review: Every single time I try to log retinol or anything with retinoids the app freezes for about 5 seconds and then crashes. I have to log everything else first and skip the retinol. This has been happening since the last update.
author: retinol_user_aus

title: The ingredient scanner is genuinely magical
review: I've been into skincare for years and I have never found a tool this good. I just point the camera at any product and within seconds I can see every ingredient flagged by concern level
date: comedogenic rating and interactions with my current routine. This feature alone is worth 5 stars.

title: Recommends products I'm literally allergic to
review: I have a documented fragrance allergy listed clearly in my profile. The app recommended me three different products this week that all contain linalool and limonene - both fragrance components I react to. This is dangerous. Please fix the allergy filtering.
author: fragrance_allergy_user

title: Morning reminder comes two hours late every day
review: My reminder is set for 6am. It arrives at 8am. I've deleted and recreated the reminder four times. I've reinstalled the app twice. It's always late. My whole point of using this app was to build a consistent morning habit.
author: routine_builder_tokyo

title: Love the scanner but recommendations need work
review: The ingredient scanner is the best I've ever used - genuinely brilliant. But the product recommendations don't match my profile at all. I have oily skin and it keeps suggesting heavy occlusive creams. The two features feel like they were built by different teams.
author: oily_skin_london

title: Lost all my progress photos after update
review: Updated to 3.2.0 and every single progress photo I'd taken over four months was gone. This was documenting my isotretinoin journey. These photos were medically significant to me. I am absolutely devastated. Restore them or I'm contacting Apple for a refund.
author: accutane_journey_user

title: Ingredient crash is reproducible every time
review: To reproduce: go to log routine
date: add any product with niacinamide first
version: then try to add anything with vitamin C. The app crashes instantly. I think it's the interaction checker that's bugging out. Please fix - I need to know if my products conflict.

title: Nighttime reminders go off in the morning
review: I set a PM reminder for 10pm skincare routine. It fires at 7am the next morning. Complete opposite of when I need it. I've checked my timezone settings multiple times. This is not a user error - something is wrong with how reminders are scheduled.
author: pm_routine_user

title: Finally an app that understands ingredient interactions
review: I'm a biochemistry student and I am genuinely impressed. The interaction warnings are accurate
version: and the scanner works even on tiny text. I recommended this to everyone in my skincare community.

title: Recommendations haven't changed in three months
review: I've been logging my routine every single day for 90 days. I've marked products as irritating
version: and ineffective. My recommendations look exactly the same as day one. The learning system is either broken or doesn't exist.

title: Photos save but then vanish after app restart
review: I take a progress photo
version: I close the app and reopen it and the photo is gone. Seems like they save to temporary storage only. I've lost maybe 20 photos this way over two months. Running iPhone 15 on iOS 17.4.

title: Scanner saved me from a terrible purchase
review: I was about to buy an expensive serum at the pharmacy. Scanned it on impulse with GlowTrack and it flagged three ingredients that conflict with my tretinoin routine. Saved me from a potentially bad reaction. This app literally paid for itself in that one moment.
author: tretinoin_user_paris

title: Crashes every time on ingredient log screen
review: I cannot log a single routine without the app crashing. Tap on Log Routine
version: tap Add Ingredient - crash. Every time. I've tried on my old iPhone and my new iPad. Same crash. I've submitted two support tickets with no response.

title: Personalization got worse after the update
review: Before 3.2 my recommendations were ok - not great but somewhat relevant. Since updating they've gotten worse. I have combination skin and now it only recommends products for dry skin. It's like my profile got reset to a default template.
author: combination_skin_user

title: Evening routine reminder fires at noon
review: I configured my evening skincare reminder for 9:30 PM. It fires at 12:03 PM instead. I've looked through every setting I can find. The time is clearly set to PM. Something is converting it to AM or just ignoring my setting entirely.
author: reminder_wrong_time

title: Best ingredient scanner on the market
review: I've tried four or five skincare apps and the ingredient scanner here is by far the best. Fast
version: and the explanation of each ingredient is written for normal people not chemists. I just wish the recommendation engine was as good.

title: Six months of progress photos gone
review: I started this app to document a serious skin condition. Six months of weekly photos representing real medical progress are now gone after the 3.2 update. I'm reporting this to Apple and looking into data recovery options. This is not acceptable.
author: medical_documentation_user

title: Recommendations completely ignore my logged reactions
review: I've marked the same type of AHA exfoliants as causing irritation four separate times. The app keeps recommending them anyway. There's clearly no feedback loop between what I log and what gets recommended. Very disappointing for a supposedly smart app.
author: feedback_ignored_user

title: Reminder reliability is hit or miss
review: Some days my reminders fire at exactly the right time and some days they're 45 minutes late. No pattern I can identify. iPhone 14 Pro
version: notifications fully enabled. The inconsistency is somehow more frustrating than if it just never worked at all.

title: App crashes when logging anything with SPF
review: Cannot log any sunscreen product. The moment I search for SPF or sunscreen in the ingredient log the app crashes to desktop. I've reported this twice. This is a core skincare step and it being broken makes the app unusable for a complete routine.
author: spf_user_miami

title: Completely changed how I shop for skincare
review: Before GlowTrack I would just guess and hope. Now I scan everything before I buy anything. The scanner has caught ingredient conflicts I never would have known about. My skin has genuinely improved since I started building evidence-based routines.
author: evidence_based_user

title: My profile recommendations are totally generic
review: I spent 20 minutes filling in my detailed skin profile - concerns
author: current routine. The recommendations I get look exactly like the generic best sellers list you'd find on any beauty blog. There's nothing personalized about them at all.
